opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The video doctor couldn't see the spots properly, and I am terrified this is meningococcal. You have to check him immediately.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Parent restarted the consultation after a video call was cut short because the camera couldn't show the child's rash clearly; parent is extremely frightened it could be meningococcal disease and wants him checked now.

symptom_narrative.onset: the parent noticed some spots on the child's tummy earlier today
symptom_narrative.character: small scattered spots on the torso; the parent is not sure exactly what they look like up close
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parent is very frightened about what the spots might mean, though the child himself seems unbothered
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly on his tummy and chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the parent has been watching them closely today
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the parent says he is otherwise running around and playing normally
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: none apparent — the child is fully active and playing

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young child at home; a parent is present and made the call and is very anxious

communication_style.typical_message_style: urgent, frightened, seeking immediate reassurance and a definitive check

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: child is running around the living room, alert, playing and interactive, moving all his arms and legs normally
a few scattered small flat spots visible on the child's tummy and chest on the video
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: video image quality is poor; fine detail and colour of the spots cannot be judged reliably over video

objective_data_offered.value: parent reports he has been drinking normally and had a wet nappy/passed urine, and is eating
